status_detail: SAME wire failure as #633. CRLF fix (DB CHAR(13,10) UPDATE → hex 4F4B0D0A 'OK\r\n', LENGTH=343) did NOT propagate to wire. Engine still TCP RST. CDP Network.loadingFailed captured exact wire error: net::ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED, canceled=false. Browser sent the POST (full cookie/auth/H2 pseudo-headers) but never received a single byte back before TCP connection closed by server. No HTTP status, no response headers, no body. DevTools panel shows streamingSearch '(failed)' status, 0.0 kB, 512ms. UI: you.com generic error '🤖 Sorry, something went wrong... Error ID: b252248e-2c39-48f5-a84a-18240f5ea0e0|...|2026-04-28T07:56:50.606Z'. Even though envelope_template is now stored correctly with CRLF, engine still fails to emit anything browser sees as valid HTTP.
request_status: NONE (no Network.responseReceived event -- connection closed before headers)
generic_error_text: 🤖 Sorry, something went wrong. Please try again later. Please include the following Error ID in your message: b252248e-2c39-48f5-a84a-18240f5ea0e0|a5f6d115-2ab3-48fa-94bf-dac485bc5d14|2026-04-28T07:56:50.606Z
progress_vs_633: SAME -- identical wire-level failure (CRLF fix did not change observable behavior)
root_cause_hypothesis: Multiple candidates (priority order from subagent diagnostic): (1) convert_to_http2_response() may still return size=0 despite fixed DB row -- cache not invalidated, wrong row read, or template not re-read after restart. (2) HTTP/2 framer rejects body when content-length missing on chunked SSE -> RST_STREAM instead of DATA frame. (3) TLS-level RST from upstream APF before HTTP layer.
recommendation_for_dev: URGENT priority: tcpdump on engine egress (port 443 to client) to confirm whether ANY HTTP/2 DATA frame leaves the box, or just RST/FIN. This will distinguish 'engine wrote bytes that client rejected' vs 'engine wrote no bytes'.

Reconfirm DB: SELECT HEX(envelope_template), LENGTH(envelope_template) FROM ...WHERE service='you'. Should start with 485454 ('HTTP') and 0D0A should appear at correct positions. Confirm engine ACTUALLY reads from this row (some engines cache or read from different row than expected).
Engine log: grep [APF:you] in /var/log/etap.log for time range 16:43-16:58 KST 2026-04-28. Did template lookup succeed? Did convert_to_http2_response return size>0?

If template-related fix doesn't work: try a known-working SSE template from chatgpt or another working service, just substitute the warning text. Validates whether SSE substitution is fundamentally broken for you.com vs whether THIS specific template is broken.

Consider: HTTP/2 implementations require either Content-Length OR chunked encoding (specifically Transfer-Encoding: chunked) for streaming. SSE on H2 typically has neither (server just sends DATA frames with END_STREAM). If engine adds Content-Length: 343 but body is variable, framer may reject.
errors_observed: you.com generic error 'Sorry, something went wrong'
notes: Cycle 95 you SSE v3 CRLF fix retry. FAIL = wire behavior unchanged from #633. Subagent 15.9min exceeded 8min hard timeout but produced higher-quality diagnostic (CDP Network domain probe + DevTools panel screenshots). Strong evidence engine still emits nothing on wire. Need tcpdump or engine-internal log next.
